Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: For what it's worth, French Kweebec is very meticulous about his work. No chunk of garlic bigger than a grain of sand is allowed to persist.

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: You watch him swirl the pestle in circles about the bowl, each seeming to intensify the aroma of garlic around the hollow. Hope that French Kweebec likes the smell -- or at least, that he has some kind of air freshener. There *are* those bushes of lavender outside.

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: The tree in question is concentrating very intently on the paste, having his reddish brows knitted together, his mouth pursed and his eyes -- those resembling a distant nebula, as is the case with Kweebecs -- following the motion of his instrument.

Line.Text: He doesn't seem winded despite the power he's pouring into the act. His breath appears to be even, and his elbow still hasn't locked in place; you heard that Kweebecs need to take special care of their joints, due to their tree-like nature.
